title: OpenClaw → Swarmstr port (Nostr-first)
Phase 0: Foundation scaffold
Define event kind/tag model and envelopes
Implement bootstrap config loader and validation
Scaffold CLI/daemon entrypoints
Phase 1: Relay runtime + DM loop
Implement relay manager (connect/reconnect/sub/pub)
Implement inbound DM subscription and decryption
Implement outbound DM send path
Phase 2: Nostr state persistence + DM policy
Implement Nostr-backed replaceable state store
Persist config/lists/sessions to Nostr
Implement DM policy modes
Phase 3: Transcript + agent integration
Persist transcript entries as parameterized docs
Integrate agent runtime loop
Add idempotency/checkpoint recovery
Phase 4: Nostr memory + local index
Design memory event schema and retrieval tags
Build local searchable cache/index
Nostr checkpoint sync for index
Phase 5: Optional admin API + parity
Implement optional local admin API
Add OpenClaw parity surface incrementally
Operational hardening and observability
Post-port enhancements (security + parity + runtime depth)
Encrypt persisted Nostr docs (NIP-44 target)
Add envelope encryption/decryption primitives for state/transcript/memory
Migrate docs repos to encrypted payloads with compatibility fallback
Harden metadata leakage policy for tags/session/source
Richer agent runtime integration
Replace echo runtime with provider-backed agent loop

notes:
Started implementation of first parity method bundle using defined gateway schemas.

notes:
Started compatibility testing phase for parity methods.
Added compatibility-focused admin method tests in internal/admin/server_test.go covering status.get response shape, memory.search normalization, strict unknown-param rejection, and config.put required-field validation.
title: Add compatibility tests against OpenClaw behavior contracts

description: Next enhancement wave focused on making Nostr the primary control/data plane: method transport over Nostr events, stronger relay-backed config/list sync semantics, and event-kind based long-term memory patterns.
notes: Program initiated with child epic swarmstr-3.1 in progress.
title: Nostr-native control plane and durable memory expansion

description: Implement request/response method execution over Nostr events (AI-Hub-aligned kinds), including correlation IDs, idempotency, auth policy, and relay routing defaults.
notes: Kickoff started: beginning design/implementation of Nostr-native method request/response transport.
title: Epic: Nostr method transport (replace local-admin-first call path)

description: Introduce a NIP-86 profile for swarmstr: method name conventions, positional params compatibility, result/error response semantics, and supportedmethods exposure; include compatibility mapping from existing method names.
notes:
Started envelope/profile alignment work: added supportedmethods method exposure and method listing in dispatcher/schema. Next: positional params compatibility and strict NIP-86 response profile mode.
Implemented substantial NIP-86 profile alignment: added supportedmethods, added positional params compatibility for key methods (array form), and added NIP-86 response profile mode ({result|error} + application/nostr+json+rpc). Updated docs accordingly.
title: Define NIP-86 compatible RPC envelope/profile for swarmstr control methods

description: Add signed-caller authorization semantics for control calls: admin pubkey list, per-method allow/deny policy, request authentication extraction, and consistent unauthorized/error behavior.
notes:
Starting with authorization-first design to align control plane with NIP-86 semantics (signed caller identity, method-level authorization, deny-by-default policy).
Added design doc docs/NIP86_ALIGNMENT_PLAN.md defining NIP-86-style semantics, auth-first policy, and phased execution for swarmstr-3.1.
Implemented auth-first control policy: added ConfigDoc.control policy model, Nostr-signed control-call auth verification (u/payload/signature/timestamp), per-method admin authorization, deny-by-default when admins configured, config validation for admin pubkeys, dispatcher enforcement before method execution, and seeded secure default control policy in runtime config. Added admin/policy tests and docs updates.
title: Implement NIP-86 style authorization model for control-plane calls

description: Implement control RPC over Nostr events using request/response kinds, request IDs, reply routing tags, timeout handling, and idempotent response publishing.
notes:
Starting Nostr-native request/response transport implementation next, using the new auth and envelope profile semantics.
Implemented Nostr-native control RPC transport: added control bus subscribing to kind 38384 addressed to daemon pubkey, request parsing and callback execution, response publish on kind 38386 with correlation tags, in-memory idempotency cache, bounded response cache, request max-age enforcement, and dedicated control checkpoint (control_ingest) wiring in swarmstrd for restart-safe dedupe.
title: Add Nostr request/response transport for control RPC with correlation + idempotency

description: Route both local HTTP /call and Nostr control events through one dispatcher and add conformance tests for NIP-86-like semantics and auth behavior parity.
notes:
Starting dual-surface conformance work next: add tests and parity checks between HTTP /call and Nostr control RPC.
Implemented dual-surface compatibility and conformance: centralized method param decoding in internal/gateway/methods, reused by HTTP /call and Nostr control handler, added tests for decoder parity/validation and control runtime helper behavior, and completed transport hardening (future-skew rejection, bounded req/cache handling, control checkpoint protection).
title: Wire dual-surface compatibility (HTTP /call + Nostr RPC) and conformance tests

acceptance_criteria:
- status alias and supported-method behavior compatible with OpenClaw clients
- chat and sessions methods accept OpenClaw parameter shapes
- response payloads match OpenClaw-required fields for core methods
- parity tests cover idempotent/in-flight/abort flows
description: Align existing core methods with OpenClaw contracts: accept OpenClaw method aliases and parameter shapes, and return compatible payload fields/lifecycle semantics (especially status/chat.send/chat.abort/chat.history/sessions.*).
notes:
Started execution: added status alias compatibility (status -> status.get) and exposed alias in supported methods; this is a first slice of core contract parity. Also added broad camelCase parameter alias normalization and tests as a foundation for chat/sessions/config contract work.
Added OpenClaw-shape compatibility for chat request decoding: chat.send accepts sessionKey/message; chat.history accepts sessionKey; chat.abort accepts sessionKey/runId. Added defensive behavior so runId-only chat.abort does not trigger global abort. Extended tests accordingly.
Completed next slice: sessions/config-adjacent contract compatibility. Added session key aliases (key/sessionKey/sessionId) across session.get and sessions.preview/patch/reset/delete/compact decoders + normalizers; sessions.preview now accepts OpenClaw keys[] and uses first key as canonical target. Added sessions.list support for OpenClaw extended fields (activeMinutes/includeGlobal/includeUnknown/includeDerivedTitles/includeLastMessage/label/spawnedBy/agentId/search) so strict decode no longer rejects these calls. Added key mirror fields in chat.abort/session mutation responses for compatibility. Expanded schema/admin tests. Validation: go test ./internal/gateway/methods ./internal/admin ./cmd/swarmstrd
Additional core-contract slice: chat.send now accepts OpenClaw idempotency/run fields and returns compatibility fields {status:"sent", run_id}. Added decode support for idempotency_key/run_id aliases and coverage in schema/admin tests.
Next slice completed: response-shape and session-surface parity improvements. sessions.list now returns OpenClaw-like envelope fields {ts,path,count,defaults,sessions}. sessions.preview now supports keys[] fanout and returns {ts,previews:[{key,status,items}]}; single-session path still returns legacy preview payload plus previews compatibility block. session.get/sessions.* decoders now accept key/sessionKey/sessionId aliases consistently. Added/updated tests in internal/admin/server_test.go and internal/gateway/methods/schema_test.go. Validation: go test ./internal/gateway/methods ./internal/admin ./cmd/swarmstrd
title: Core method contract parity for status/chat/sessions/config
